steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 1: Greenidge-Sabral wanted to avoid harsh synthetic dyes. Sentence 2: She experimented with natural dye methods. The second sentence describes the action she took in response to the goal stated in sentence 1 — a motivation-to-action relationship. The transition must signal 'acting on the prior idea.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — With this in mind,: Signals 'motivated by what was just stated' — exactly the cause-to-action link needed here. ✓
  • Choice B — To sum up,: Introduces a conclusion or summary of multiple preceding points. There is only one prior sentence, and the second is not a summary. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: Signals contrast or concession. The second sentence reinforces the first rather than contrasting it. ✗
  • Choice D — In actuality,: Signals a correction of a misconception. No misconception has been stated. ✗
That matches Choice A — With this in mind,.
